one_liner: SPY down 0.72% with RSI at 39 in bearish MACD territory — broad market in neutral-to-bearish consolidation; watch $653 support, no structural entry signal yet.

news_catalyst.headline: S&P 500 Market at Tipping Point — Rally Rotation Underway Per IBD
news_catalyst.summary: The broader S&P 500 (via SPY) is down 0.72% with RSI at 39.1 approaching oversold territory. IBD noted the market is 'at a tipping point' with a rally rotation into select sectors (healthcare, energy). The market is in Wyckoff neutral consolidation with bearish MACD — not a favorable macro environment for aggressive long exposure. SPY's $653 support is 10% below current levels — meaningful drawdown risk if economic data disappoints.

technical.summary: SPY in neutral Wyckoff consolidation with no structural signals. RSI at 39 approaching oversold, bearish MACD persists. The broad market macro context is cautious — no clear accumulation phase. Use SPY as a market regime indicator: a reclaim of RSI>50 and MACD bullish crossover would signal a return to risk-on conditions across the watchlist.

key_concerns: Bearish technical regime at current reading
fundamentals.summary: SPY as a market regime indicator: Grade C reflects a mixed economic environment. The 'rally rotation' noted by IBD suggests selective opportunities in healthcare and energy, not broad market exposure.

risks: RSI 39 and bearish MACD — broad market in a weak technical regime
10% downside to $653 support if macro deteriorates
Bearish MACD crossover in place — no confirmation of trend reversal yet
